premise: I spent seven years as the sole caregiver for my elderly in-laws, pouring $93,000 of my own savings into renovating their lakefront cabin, while my brother-in-law visited once a year. When the last parent died, the will left the cabin solely to him, and he served me an eviction notice on the day of the funeral — I proved the final will was signed while my mother-in-law was heavily medicated, won an undue influence suit, reclaimed the cabin, and won a $93,000 reimbursement judgment that forced him to sell.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: He Evicted Me At The Funeral After Getting The Lake Cabin I Renovated. So I Watc
